metadata.title: Flexible Attention-Based Multi-Policy Fusion for Efficient Deep Reinforcement Learning

sections.title: Why Knowledge-Grounded Reinforcement Learning?
content: What learning capabilitiesdo humans possess, yet RL agents still missing? [1,2]
Compositional:Combine policies from multiple sources to form a knowledge set.
Incremental:Arbitrarily remove outdated policies or add new ones.
Generalizable:Be able to apply previously learned policies to unseen tasks.
KGRL:An RL paradigm aiming to learn with external policies and achieve the five properties above.

sections.title: Method: Knowledge-Inclusive Attention Network (KIAN)
content: KIAN:An actor that separates knowledge representations from their selection mechanism.
Naive policy fusion leads to challenges:
Entropy Imbalance in maximum entropy KGRL
